channel.title: Communication Skills on MinWeb
channel.description: Recent content in Communication Skills on MinWeb

item.title: Why You Should Stop Saying "We Need to Talk
item.description:
Have you ever received a text that simply said, “We need to talk,” and felt your stomach drop into your shoes?

Your heart starts racing. Your palms get sweaty. You spend the next three hours spiraling, convinced you’re getting fired, being broken up with, or that the world is ending.

If this happens to you, you aren’t alone. This phrase has earned a reputation as the “four-word disaster.” Honestly, it’s the most effective way to guarantee a conversation starts in a place of panic, defensiveness, and unnecessary emotional carnage.

item.title: When Texting Feels Cold: Should You Make the Call?
item.description:
Have you ever stared at your phone, heart rate climbing, trying to figure out if that short, punctuation-free text was playful or just cold? You read it once, then again. You type out a reply, delete it, and start over.

We’ve all been there. Texting is efficient, but it’s a minefield of ambiguity. A 2005 study by Kruger et al. found that people overestimate their ability to convey sarcasm or emotion over text by a staggering 30%. When that “digital silence”—the gap between sent messages—starts to feel like a frost settling over the conversation, the anxiety kicks in.
